purpose: Reference snapshot of Apple-documented App Store Connect role capabilities and API-key constraints. This file is for agent/runtime reference, not authorization logic.
limitations: Exact role lookup comes from the live App Store Connect web session, but the expanded capabilities below come from this bundled Apple documentation snapshot.
Apple documents some permissions as conditional, separately granted, or account-state dependent, so role mapping is not endpoint-by-endpoint proof of access.

team_keys.team_scope: Team API keys apply across all apps. Apple says app access cannot be limited for a team API key.

individual_keys.default_generation_permission: All App Store Connect users can generate and revoke their own individual API key unless an Admin or Account Holder revokes the Generate Individual API Keys permission.

capability_groups.label: Request App Store Connect API access
capability_groups.summary: Apple documents this as Account Holder only.

capability_groups.label: Generate team API keys
capability_groups.summary: Apple documents this as Account Holder or Admin.

capability_groups.label: View and manage all team and individual API keys
capability_groups.summary: Apple documents this as Account Holder or Admin.

capability_groups.label: Generate individual API keys
capability_groups.summary: Allowed for eligible App Store Connect users unless an Admin or Account Holder revokes the Generate Individual API Keys permission.

capability_groups.label: Access to all apps
capability_groups.summary: Admins have access to all apps. Finance can view all apps in reporting surfaces.

capability_groups.label: Manage app pricing and App Store information
capability_groups.summary: Examples from Apple's role permissions page include setting app price, scheduling price changes, and creating a new version.

capability_groups.label: Manage marketing materials and promotional artwork
Analyze and respond to customer reviews
capability_groups.summary: Role permissions page includes viewing ratings and reviews and responding to reviews.

capability_groups.label: View App Analytics
capability_groups.summary: Examples from Apple's role permissions page include viewing app metrics, acquisition sources, retention, benchmarks, and downloading App Analytics reports.

capability_groups.label: View Sales and Trends
capability_groups.summary: Examples from Apple's role permissions page include viewing units, proceeds, sales, pre-orders, subscription data, and downloading reports.

capability_groups.label: Payments, financial reports, and tax forms
Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles by default
capability_groups.summary: Apple explicitly says Admins on organization teams have Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles by default.

capability_groups.label: Legal and membership-sensitive actions
capability_groups.summary: Apple documents these as Account Holder only: signing legal agreements, renewing membership, requesting API access, removing auto-renewable subscriptions from sale, submitting Safari Extensions, and creating Developer ID certificates.

roles.label: Account Holder
roles.summary: Primary team contact with access to all aspects of App Store Connect, including legal agreements with Apple.
notes: Apple's current roles documentation says the Account Holder can access all aspects of App Store Connect.
notable_exclusive_actions: Request access to the App Store Connect API

roles.label: Admin
roles.summary: Secondary team contact with access to all apps and many of the same responsibilities as the Account Holder.
notes: Apple says Admins on organization teams have Certificates, Identifiers & Profiles by default.

roles.label: Finance
roles.summary: Manages financial information, including downloading reports and uploading tax forms.
notes: Apple says Finance can view all apps in Payments and Financial Reports, Sales and Trends, and App Analytics.

roles.label: App Manager
roles.summary: Manages all aspects of an app, including pricing, App Store information, and app development and delivery.
example_tasks: Release a version update in phases

roles.label: Developer
roles.summary: Manages development and delivery of an app.

roles.label: Marketing
roles.summary: Manages marketing materials and promotional artwork and may be contacted by Apple about featuring.

roles.label: Sales
roles.summary: Analyzes sales, downloads, and other analytics for an app.

roles.label: Customer Support
roles.summary: Analyzes and responds to customer reviews on the App Store.
